name: Telomerase Restoration Coverage Repair Assessment for June 10, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 10 surveillance assessment requested recent-review, registry-watchlist, safety, and taxonomy-boundary repair before ordinary surveillance resumed.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for the current tentative human_biomarker_signal public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages the 2025 TA-65 systematic review and meta-analysis, no-results TA-65 and telomerase-activator registry records, disease-context Libella AAV-hTERT registry boundaries, TERT mRNA radiation-injury boundary context, and a refreshed Telomerase Restoration evidence map. Coverage is not strong because long-term telomerase safety, human function, clinical outcomes, and gene-therapy results remain missing.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public/staged map now includes TA-65 randomized trials, the uncontrolled TA-65 health-maintenance program, the 2025 TA-65 systematic review, TAT2 ex vivo human-cell evidence, the Libella AAV-hTERT aging registry, two disease-context Libella AAV-hTERT registries, and the TA-65 or telomerase-activator registry watchlist.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This is enough for the current biomarker-stage public claim, but not for human clinical benefit, broad healthy-aging claims, or gene-therapy safety.

evidence_categories.rationale: The map includes the uncontrolled health-maintenance program as confounded human context. Broader observational telomere-length or telomerase-activity cohorts were not deeply mapped because they do not directly test telomerase-restoration interventions.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This category is not required for the current public claim, but future taxonomy work could decide whether specific observational telomerase/telomere maintenance cohorts add useful boundary context.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline already includes inducible TERT reactivation in telomerase-deficient mice, AAV9-mTERT lifespan evidence in adult and old mice, and TA-65 adult/old mouse healthspan evidence, while separately flagging the CMV-vector TERT lifespan retraction.

Biology coverage now includes telomerase reactivation, AAV9-mTERT delivery, TA-65 telomere and immune-biomarker biology, TAT2 ex vivo telomerase activation, no-results AAV-hTERT registry endpoints, and TERT mRNA radiation-injury context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: TERT mRNA remains boundary context because it did not extend telomeres during the experiment and was not an aging intervention result.

evidence_categories.rationale: The map now captures the null primary CD8+ TEMRA endpoint in the post-MI TA-65 trial, uncontrolled-program confounding, the 2025 review's telomere-function disconnect, no-results TA-65/telomerase-activator registries, no-results AAV-hTERT registries, TERT mRNA non-aging boundary context, and the CMV-vector TERT retraction.

The staged map represents the key safety limitation: short human TA-65 safety windows, mild gastrointestinal events in the review, unresolved longer-term cancer risk, and no posted AAV-hTERT human safety results.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This is adequate coverage of the limitation, not evidence that the limitation is resolved.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged repair represents the identified registry set: the existing Libella AAV-hTERT aging registry, two disease-context Libella AAV-hTERT registries, three historical TA-65 registries, one cycloastragenol disease-boundary registry, and the current planned TA-65 vascular-function registry.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue ordinary surveillance for status changes, posted registry results, or linked publications.

evidence_categories.rationale: PMID 41286474 is staged as the direct recent TA-65 systematic review/meta-analysis, and PMID 41002620 was screened but excluded as less direct Astragalus vascular-aging context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future reviews may add context, but the named high-priority review gap is repaired.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes key telomerase-deficient mouse rescue, AAV9-mTERT mouse lifespan, TA-65 mouse and human biomarker, TAT2 ex vivo, AAV-hTERT registry, and CMV-vector retraction anchors; the repair adds modern TA-65 review triangulation without displacing those anchors.

The repair now separates direct TA-65 biomarker evidence, telomerase-activator disease-boundary context, no-results AAV-hTERT disease registries, TERT mRNA radiation-injury biology, and telomere-length-only nonintervention records.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future taxonomy work may still split telomere protection, telomerase activation, and DNA-repair-related TERT mRNA claims more finely, but the current public track boundary is adequate.

search_log_summary: Coverage repair used NCBI PubMed ESummary/EFetch for the named TA-65 meta-analysis, TERT mRNA boundary paper, and Astragalus vascular-aging review; ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for all named TA-65, cycloastragenol, and Libella AAV-hTERT registries; and local evidence-map comparison against the latest published outlook and the June 10 surveillance coverage assessment.
source_selection_notes: PMID 41286474 was staged because it directly repairs the missing TA-65 systematic review/meta-analysis gap.
The TA-65 and telomerase-activator registry set was staged because future surveillance needs concrete no-results records rather than an implicit watchlist.
NCT02530255 is represented as cycloastragenol disease-boundary context because the API does not identify TA-65 as the intervention.
PMID 40958415 was staged as TERT mRNA boundary context because it reports no telomere extension during the experiment and does not test aging outcomes.
PMID 41002620 was screened but excluded as context-only because the direct TA-65 synthesis and registry records better addressed the assessment gaps.

known_gaps.description: Long-term human safety evidence for telomerase activation or gene therapy remains absent, especially cancer surveillance, clonal expansion, and persistence of effect after treatment.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public read tentative until independent long-term safety evidence appears.

known_gaps.description: Human studies still have not shown repeated, prespecified improvements in function, disease outcomes, or survival tied to telomerase or telomere restoration.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Watch for independent TA-65, telomerase-activator, or telomerase-therapy trials with prespecified function or clinical outcomes.

known_gaps.description: The aging-directed and disease-context AAV-hTERT registries have no posted results, leaving human gene-therapy safety and target-change claims unsupported.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Continue ClinicalTrials.gov and publication surveillance for NCT04133649, NCT04110964, and NCT04133454.

known_gaps.description: The repaired TA-65/telomerase-activator registry set still has no posted results, including completed, terminated, unknown-status, and planned records.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Re-check registry result sections and linked publications during ordinary surveillance.

known_gaps.description: Observational telomere-length, telomerase-activity, stress, nutrition, and disease-boundary cohorts remain outside the present intervention-focused map.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Only expand this category if a future taxonomy pass decides observational cohorts materially clarify intervention boundaries.

next_coverage_action: Run evidence review and editorial review for the staged coverage-repair bundle. If approved and published, return Telomerase Restoration to ordinary surveillance with special watch on TA-65 registry results, Libella AAV-hTERT registry results, TERT mRNA boundary updates, and long-term safety signals.
